new_risk_factors: Removal of geopolitical conflict risk disclosures (Russia-Ukraine, Israel/Middle East) — notable absence suggesting management no longer deems these material enough to warrant explicit hedging language
Removal of foreign currency exchange rate risk language — may indicate reduced international revenue exposure or FX hedging confidence
key_quotes: the ongoing Russia-Ukraine, and Israel and broader Middle East conflicts are still evolving and the outcomes remain highly uncertain
we do not expect that the resulting challenging macroeconomic conditions will have a material impact on our business or results of operations

If the respective conflicts continue or worsen, leading to greater disruptions and uncertainty within the technology industry or global economy, our business and results of operations could be negatively impacted
Exchange rates are subject to significant and rapid fluctuations due to a number of factors
payload.mda_summary: The delta is entirely driven by removed language: PLTR eliminated its boilerplate geopolitical conflict disclaimers (Russia-Ukraine, Israel/Middle East) and foreign currency risk language, with nothing new added in their place. The removal of the reassuring 'we do not expect material impact' geopolitical hedge could be interpreted two ways — either management views these risks as sufficiently diminished to drop the disclosure, or international operations have become less material. The absence of new language makes this a low-signal quarter with no meaningful leading indicator content; analysts are unlikely to revise estimates based on this delta alone.
